programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest, presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A fun game in the pub takes an unexpected turn when Isaac shows his true colours to Patrick. Chantelle feels the pressure to find work and Mitch's suspicions about Gray reach fever pitch. Mick is proud of Linda for going to another meeting but when Stuart spots her he realises how much she is struggling(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Restaurant critic William Sitwell sets the brief for the third quarter-final, challenging the heat-winners to serve up a plant-based dish. John Torode and Gregg Wallace then decide who will be progressing further in the competition and who will be hanging up their aprons(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mitch follows Gray to find out what he is up to - only to be stopped in his tracks by Chantelle, who warns him to back off. Stuart comes up with an extreme to cheer up Rainie - all he needs now is a ring - and Sheree berates Isaac for his attitude towards Patrick(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: The Hadleys and the McGregors both attend Callum's passing-in parade to the military academy, where Meggie sees a familiar face from the past and Kamal makes a controversial speech to the assembled crowd. Callum and Sephy snatch a moment together and realise they must find a place that they can be alone together - but Lekan suspects Sephy is hiding something from him and is on the rampage. Meanwhile, Jude is getting involved deeper with Dorn and his ambitions to make himself the new leader of terrorist group the Liberation Militia(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from West Bromwich, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason, Katya Adler and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: In Lancashire, Michelle Ackerley learns how drone technology is helping police crack down on wildlife criminals and trace missing people. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers want to improve the planning permission in place on a terrace in Borough Green in Kent, while a mother and daughter hope they don't have too much to do on their first investment in Liverpool. Plus, a brother and sister who are seasoned property developers have ambitious plans for a generous end-of-terrace in south London's Streatham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood lines up with some serious firepower, working as a US marshal in San Antonio, Texas. His mentor is Deputy Chris Bozeman, who's spent the last 15 years arresting dangerous and wanted men. After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A plane crash in Quebec leaves a pilot stranded, an injured hiker drags himself three kilometres to safety in the Australian Outback and a brother saves his sibling who has lost consciousness on a free dive in the Bahamas. Narrated by Kevin Duala(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special edition in aid of Sport Relief in which the reds and blues are made up of Olympic legends Iwan Thomas and Tessa Sanderson, who face BBC sport presenters Manish Bhasin and John Watson. Eric Knowles hosts, while experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian offer their advice. This show features the debut of the very first Bargain Hunt games, where antique experts go head to compete for BH Sport Relief Cup. Iwan also finds time to also pay a visit to the Change Foundation, a charity that runs the London Futures project which is supported by Sport Relief.(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma comes under fire from the police in charge of Jimmi's case, while Al plays bad cop. Ayesha and Sid help when a patient's vasectomy leads to some shocking revelations(n)

programme.title: Five Bedrooms
programme.desc: Money worries overwhelm Liz, posing a threat to the potentially loved-up household. Drama, Starring Kat Stewart, Stephen Peacocke and Doris Younane(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in the Shropshire countryside to help a couple who have £425,000 to invest in a character home that gives them space for entertaining and their beloved jukebox. Away from the house search, Ginny visits the Royal Air Force Museum Cosford and meets the conservation team reconstructing a rare Hampden bomber which played an important role in the skies during the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades searches for three items he can rework to reveal their true potential. To help him on his quest, he enlists woodworker Ollie Allen and upholsterer Sharon O'Connor. Ollie has his hands full with a rickety old shelving unit, while Sharon needs to come up with a bright idea for a pair of garden chairs. Jay himself attempts to transform an unwanted side table(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Matched up with chef Ellis Barrie, loved-up bride-to-be Daniella is keen to rustle up some mouth-watering pasta dishes inspired by her Italian heritage. In the green pepper kitchen, budding groom Ross is paired with chef Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Paddy McGuinness, Alex Scott, Emma Willis, Rylan Clark-Neal, Oti Mabuse and Tom Allen host the extravaganza, raising funds for good causes both in the UK and overseas. Highlights include two teams of celebrities leaving it all on the dance floor in a bid to be crowned The Greatest Dancer for Sport Relief and the rebirth of Killing Eve as Killing Steves, for a one-off Sport Relief spoof. The Battle of the Channels Celebrity Boat Race returns, and this year it's bigger than ever as BBC, ITV, Channel 4 and Sky compete for victory. In another sporting feat, Romesh Ranganathan takes on tennis legend Andy Murray in a crazy game of crazy golf. Plus, live music from artists including Rita Ora, John Newman and Celeste(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Fundraising fun, live from Media City in Salford. Tom Allen and Maya Jama see viewers through to the early hours, joined by dance sensations AJ and Curtis Pritchard to get the nation up on their feet. YouTuber KSI performs a world exclusive of his single Wake Up Call, while rising star Celeste performs her new single Strange. Plus, a look back at last summer's All-Star Sport Relief Netball match with sports captains Oti Mabuse and Jennifer Saunders, a sketch with the cast of the Netflix smash hit Stranger Things and the final round of A Question of Sport Relief(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team are at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where they restore a broken games table, a pair of old lamps and a tired display cabinet(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Anna Haugh and Akis Petretzikis create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought in by a couple who have developed a renewed interest in healthy eating. With Rylan Clark-Neal(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the county line from Devon into Somerset, seeing first-hand how willow farmers sought to overcome the challenge from the production of synthetic plastics during the 1930s and hearing how tourism has spread across the region, especially amongst the young. In Bath, Michael visits the former home of a refugee emperor, whose country was invaded by the Italian dictator Mussolini, and in whose name a religious movement began that now flourishes worldwide. Travelling east into Wiltshire, he reaches the largest training area of the British Army, Salisbury Plain(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar walks through the Bolton Abbey Estate, heading out from Bolton Priory along the River Wharfe and over moorland to her journey's end at Simon's Seat and its views across Wharfedale, Nidderdale and beyond. Meeting fellow walkers along the way, Shanaz finds inspirational spots to sketch and recite poetry, while discovering hidden woodlands and waterfalls(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil presents live coverage of Chancellor Rishi Sunak's 2020 Budget with expert analysis from Laura Kuenssberg, Faisal Islam and Simon Jack. Includes Prime Minister's Questions at noon(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Dunne needs to damage Aoife's credibility, Patricia may need to stomach an illicit solution for her faulty flats and Bridget is bothered in her own home(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: In the second round of this heat, the three remaining designers take on one of the most demanding rooms to redesign on a tight budget - the kitchen. They're each working in a Victorian Half-House, in Walthamstow, North East London, where architectural historian Tom Dyckhoff looks at the story behind these early apartments, and the family who built them(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his search through the National Archives, unearthing files outlining the state's strategy on Scientology, exploring how bull running was outlawed in Britain, and taking a look at pictures by John Lennon that were banned(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Huddersfield Town Hall, West Yorkshire, Nick Davies is drawn to a bobbin holder that reveals the town's textile heritage, while Christina Trevanion finds an incredible collection of glass scent bottles. Presenter, Paul Martin delves into the area's coal mining history(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the capital on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore London's new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels around the firths of Easter Ross and Sutherland, beginning at Loch Ussie - famous for its connections with the Brahan Seer, who predicted future calamities. On a golf course above Strathpeffer, he discovers an unlikely connection between the conventional old spa town below and occultist and author Aleister Crowley, before heading for the Dornoch Firth, where he encounters a witch and a fairy bridge(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A crew of RNLI volunteers in Newquay is called out to a shipwreck off the Cornish coast, where a diver who has lost his mouthpiece needs rescuing from 30 metres underwater. A yacht in the Dover Strait starts to sink - and may collide with a cargo ship before going under - and a Dorset crew search for a windsurfer who has got caught in a storm(n)

programme.title: Child of Our Time
programme.desc: In 1999, the groundbreaking BBC series Child of Our Time began filming a group of babies from the moment they were born, to explore what would shape their lives in the new millennium. Twenty years on ,this programme catches us with Eve, Jamie and Rhianna from the project, with the three reflecting on growing up during a time of extraordinary social change(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sitcom star and The Last Leg regular Josh Widdicombe plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He'll be warming up the audience and then introducing routines by Londoner Nathan Caton and Celia Pacquola from Australia(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs pitch their money-making ideas to a panel of self-made millionaires, who are prepared to invest their company's money in the scheme if they think it could work(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: The final episode of the series comes from Bolton, a city with unusually high levels of unemployment for people between 18 and 25. Waitress Jenny works on a zero hour contract, and since universal credit is paid a month in arrears, she finds it very difficult to predict how much money she will have to live off at any given time. New claimant Paula is given an advance payment to ease her through the five-week transition period to universal credit, but finds herself struggling to pay it back and live on her monthly payments(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: At Aintree Hospital's major trauma centre, a 15-strong team try to save the life of a young man who has arrived by emergency helicopter. He has been stabbed in the chest - one of three stabbings to arrive during the day. Across the city at the Royal Liverpool University Hospital, staff deal with a homeless man who has been returning for at least 18 years. He is always treated with patience and dignity and given somewhere to sleep his alcohol or substance misuse. However, his increasingly aggressive behaviour leads to him being issued with an ultimatum(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades salvages three items from a recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, including a collection of slate tiles and a trio of metal lights(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: City-dwelling families, couples and individuals set out to find their ideal rural retreats with the help of a property expert(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the east of England in the inter-war period, with the first leg of his journey beginning in Canterbury and ending at Alexandra Palace in London(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In the final episode, the chef and the group of volunteers discover how much weight they have lost over the past two months, and how much fitter they are as a result of all their hard work. To celebrate their achievements, Tom decides to throw a special picnic by the river, and whips up a series of exciting, healthy recipes they can all enjoy(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in Leicester - a 100-year-old Dutch Barge, a modern mansion and a 'Scandinavian barn'-inspired rebuild(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their tour around Britain, following Samuel Chamberlain's 1963 travelogue British Bouquet: An Epicurean Tour of Britain. This leg takes them to Bristol, where they rub shoulders with Elizabethan pirates and learn about an ancient system of cultivation that uses fish waste as fertiliser. They also find time to cook up some gujarati street food, take a trip back to the 1970s and help decorate a chocolate version of the SS Great Britain(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Members of the Kiely family face a problem. Johno reckons Mick Moran was involved in Keith's disappearance, so Mick needs an alibi from Alice(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The two remaining designers compete for a place in the quarter final, this time transforming two attic bedrooms in desirable 1960s riverside homes in Marlow, Buckinghamshire. Each contestant is given a very different brief, with one designer facing the challenge of delivering a modern bachelor pad style room for the homeowners' son. The other must create a hippy yet chic bedroom inspired by the colours of an Indian sari, but their decision to use a multi-coloured scheme and create bulky pieces of bespoke furniture casts doubt on their ability. Presented by Tom Dyckhoff(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his search through previously classified documents in the National Archives, learning how Mary Whitehouse defended God in a court of law. He also discloses details of the last woman to be imprisoned under the Witchcraft Acts, and unearths files that reveal the Church's role in slavery(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Piccadilly Circus to Gravesend, visiting an exotic West End store that was popular among Victorian women and hearing how a Russian ballet company took London by storm in 1909. He also discovers the origins of netball and gymslips at North Kent College and meets the pilots of the Port of London Authority, founded in the early 20th century(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Lindisfarne, which lies off the Northumberland coast. From what became known as the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, monks spread Christianity to the rest of northern England and created one of Britain's finest treasures - the Lindisfarne Gospels. Viking attacks caused the monks to flee but the village survived and today the community has to share it with thousands of tourists(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: A customised 1962 Chevy Impala has arrived at the Victoria & Albert for a new exhibition dedicated to cars. But this unique vehicle has come all the way from Los Angeles and as if that move was not tough enough, Catherine Sargent is now concerned it will be a tight fit getting such a large car into the exhibition space. Curator Alice Minter has chosen a table with a micro-mosaic top as part of an exhibition to tour the world, but it will need some serious restoration work before it can go anywhere, and a rare medieval jewel undergoes a deep clean(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on medically fit patients stranded in hospital due to a lack of after-care. They include a 70-year-old vascular dementia patient who is stuck in Aintree University Hospital until he and his wife can find a residential home to deal with his complex needs. Plus, the story of a three-year-old with a rare genetic disease who has been waiting in hospital for eight months while his local council take the extraordinary decision to build him and his family an entire house equipped with everything the youngster requires(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Vic presents Bob with a striking new wig, before revealing his secret beat boxing skills. Plus, the return of the Man with the Stick, and sketches including the Free Runners and Frank Roddam's latest inventions. Comedy series with Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer, featuring sketches, slapstick, songs and stunts, accompanied by a series of special guests.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Miriam's Big Fat Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. Actress Miriam Margolyes travels around the nation to discover why so many people are overweight, despite so much information about healthy lifestyles. She wants to understand what impact being obese has on people and how the rest of society views them and begins by meeting people fighting back against their size - both physically and psychologically(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dom helps track down fugitives with a bounty on their heads as he works as a private investigator in Waco, Texas. His mentor is James Fullner, a private investigator who's spent 15 years in the business, exposing cheating partners and business fraud, and apprehending fugitives on the run. Working alongside an armed team of investigators, on behalf of a local bail bondsman, they hit the streets in pursuit of a violent fugitive, a runaway drug addict and a wanted sex offender(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: In the final episode, Michael Mosley investigates whether simply thinking about food can help you lose weight, and Giles Yeo finds out how to keep your skin young and healthy. Meanwhile, Alain Gregoire tests whether listening to music improves your sleep, and Guddi Singh examines the latest diet in the headlines which claims to have a good impact on the planet(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves old tents, four dining chairs and a bamboo dressing screen from being thrown in the tip, hoping she and two others can make money by restoring the items(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts as mother and daughter Audrey and Candice take on the light-hearted culinary challenge, with the help of chefs Ellis Barrie and Mike Reid(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: A family of five works in a corner shop to reveal how such businesses have changed over the course of the past 100 years, aided by presenter Sara Cox and social historian Polly Russell. The first episode covers shops from the Victorian era to the First World War. The family must learn to bake their own counter goods, measure loose products like tea, flour and sugar by hand, and make deliveries with a horse and cart. They also receive a visit from Kelly Holmes, who puts them through an Edwardian workout and discover the impact of food shortages on corner shops as the war drew on(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day, with reports from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three semi-detached properties in Leicester - including a shabby chic family home, and an Edwardian property with some rather eclectic interiors - are in the spotlight(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey around the nation with a visit to Tunbridge Wells in Kent. They take a stroll down the historic Pantiles, an old Regency arcade filled with food shops - including one for very discerning dogs. They also have a cookery lesson from Rosemary Shrager and visit a hop farm(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Kevin Dunne fears the framing of Doyle has failed, while the Kielys may finally be free of Eugene Casey and Johno is jealous of Patricia's new partner(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The fourth and final heat sees four more amateur designers take up the challenge as they each attempt to transform the bedroom of a house on an unusual self-build estate in south London, with three days, £1,000 and a small team of assistants to help deliver their projects. Tom Dyckhoff examines the history of the neighbourhood, before judges Sophie Robinson and Daniel Hopwood decide which contender is to leave the competition(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo concludes his search through the National Archives, unearthing memos relating to one of the SAS's most daring raids and documents pertaining to a conspiracy to overthrow the government. He also looks at how Churchill thought the unthinkable in the wake of defeating the Nazis(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts James Lewis and Catherine Southon search for items to take off to auction at Dover Castle in Kent. James unearths a silver owl inkwell and Catherine discovers an alabaster bust. Presenter Paul Martin delves into the history of the English Channel(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In the orchards of East Malling, Kent, Michael discovers how the Edwardians' serious attitude towards cultivation bears fruit even now. In Folkestone, he hears how the town coped with an influx of more than 100,000 refugees fleeing the German invasion in 1914. In the High Weald, Michael heads for Tenterden Town and the light railway that opened in 1900. His final stop is the medieval Cinque Port of Rye, where he tours the home and garden of author Henry James(n)

programme.title: Extreme Wales with Richard Parks
programme.desc: The presenter explores the River Teifi, taking part in adrenaline-filled activities such as tackling rapids and travelling at speed on open water. Richard then heads out to spend time meeting those who live on the banks of the river, and finds out why these adventures are so important to the Welsh people(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef takes a culinary tour of France to discover what Britain's nearest neighbours have to teach us. He begins his trip in Dieppe, where he prepares freshly caught turbot, forages for sea herbs along the Bay of the Somme, and cooks a seafood gratin. He then heads for southern Champagne, a region that was a favourite of Renoir(n)

programme.title: Wild Cuba: A Caribbean Journey
programme.desc: In the last of his two-part series, cameraman, director and naturalist Colin Stafford-Johnson encounters bats emerging from a labyrinth of limestone caves to feed on nectar from giant hibiscus flowers, and tiny frogs, smaller than a fingernail, living in patches of hidden emerald forest. Meanwhile, on the white sand beaches, baby green turtles emerge from their nests to make their way to the Caribbean sea. Colin also sees how the modern world is impacting this island paradise, meeting endangered Cuban Crocodiles as well as parrots under pressure from habitat loss(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Aisling Bea, Joe Lycett and David Mitchell join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the oceans. One of the questions touches on one publisher's suggestion for an improvement to Moby Dick. As ever, the quiz sees correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: A special, fundraising edition of A Question of Sport, with Sue Barker at the helm. Joining the panel alongside captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are Mel Giedroyc, Tom Davis, Chris Kamara and Paddy McGuinness(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Katie Razzall reviews the day's important national and international news stories(n)

programme.title: Miriam's Big Fat Adventure
programme.desc: Part two of two. Miriam Margolyes sets out to find out what is being done to combat the obesity crisis, and meets people who are going to extreme and sometimes controversial lengths to lose weight. To understand the true cost of the epidemic, Miriam meets 41-year-old Angela, whose leg was amputated two years ago. Her weight meant she needed a knee operation, which led to a sepsis infection. Stuck at home relying on carers to wash, dress and feed her, Miriam is shocked to learn how Angela's weight upended her life(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade on the Silk Road
programme.desc: Documentary series focusing on the traditional crafts along the ancient trade route of the Silk Road, capturing the techniques used by master craftspeople at work. The first edition is set in North West China, where the Uyghur community has been making Atlas silk for thousands of years. It follows a weaver, Mattursun Islam, and his family as they continue the tradition and produce silk in over 200 designs in a small workshop(n)

programme.title: The Victorian House of Arts and Crafts
programme.desc: Using original tools and techniques, the six 21st-century crafters are set to make from scratch a Victorian double bed and bed spread, a bedside clock and plaster-wall decoration in a week, all the while eating, working and living within the philosophies first outlined by the likes of John Ruskin and William Morris. Will their 1890s communal life help them to better understand the depth and scale of the arts and crafts movement, both as a power for artistic and social change? Once again, host Anita Rani is joined by contributors Keith Brymer Jones and Patch Rogers(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: England Women v Spain Women (Kick-off 9.15pm). Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of both teams' concluding match of the round-robin competition, held at Toyota Stadium in Frisco, Texas. England prevailed 2-1 when the sides last met ahead of the 2019 World Cup courtesy of goals from Beth Mead and Ellen White, and another closely contested match is expected tonight(n)

programme.title: The Making of Spain
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore examines Spain's Golden Age under Philip II through to the Spanish Civil War and dictatorship under Franco, from which Spain has emerged as a modern democratic monarchy(n)

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Greece
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke traces the complex and fascinating story of Western civilisation's love affair with Greek art, learning how people in succeeding centuries found inspiration for their own ideals and ambitions. Filming in Britain, France, Italy and Germany, Alastair visits the Aphrodite of Knidos, which was the first naked woman in Western art; the bronze horses of St Mark's in Venice, which became a pawn in an imperial game; and the Discobolus, which was purchased by Nazi leader Adolf Hitler(n)

programme.title: Anni Albers: A Life in Thread
programme.desc: The story of Anni Albers, one of the female Bauhaus pioneers who transformed the perception of textiles into modern art. In 1922, Anni enrolled at the legendary Bauhaus school of art in Weimar Germany to become a painter, but was soon moved into the weaving class which became known as the women's workshop. Here, she joined a group of women who turned the looms to their advantage and used them to create a radical new kind of modern art-textile abstraction. The film also documents Anni's later life in America, where she became one of the world's most famous living artists(n)

programme.title: Handmade on the Silk Road
programme.desc: Documentary series focusing on the traditional crafts along the ancient trade route of the Silk Road, capturing the techniques used by master craftspeople at work. The first edition is set in North West China, where the Uyghur community has been making Atlas silk for thousands of years. It follows a weaver, Mattursun Islam, and his family as they continue the tradition and produce silk in over 200 designs in a small workshop(n)

programme.title: The Victorian House of Arts and Crafts
programme.desc: Using original tools and techniques, the six 21st-century crafters are set to make from scratch a Victorian double bed and bed spread, a bedside clock and plaster-wall decoration in a week, all the while eating, working and living within the philosophies first outlined by the likes of John Ruskin and William Morris. Will their 1890s communal life help them to better understand the depth and scale of the arts and crafts movement, both as a power for artistic and social change? Once again, host Anita Rani is joined by contributors Keith Brymer Jones and Patch Rogers(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary making use of scientific research into animal anatomy to explain how various species are uniquely built to thrive in specific habitats. Chris Packham begins by examine details the penguin, detailing how the aquatic, flightless bird's legs, wings and body shape have allowed it to conquer a wide range of landscapes, from deep forests and tropical waters to bustling cities and the toughest place on the planet - Antarctica(n)

programme.title: Shipwrecks: Britain's Sunken History
programme.desc: Maritime historian Dr Sam Willis reveals the stories behind some of the shipwrecks that surround Britain's coastline. He begins by examining how the top-heavy Mary Rose became a source of national embarrassment and discovers why the wrecking of the Spanish Armada was so unexpected. He also considers the terrifying real-life disasters that inspired two great castaway tales - The Tempest by William Shakespeare and Daniel Defoe's Robinson Crusoe(n)

programme.title: Can Science Make Me Perfect? with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on an audacious scientific stunt - to rebuild her own body from scratch, editing out errors left behind by evolution, to create the Perfect Body. Alice finds out what the body's biggest problems are and how amazing adaptations in the rest of the animal kingdom could provide inspiration for her project, before unveiling her life-size model in front of 150 people at London's Science Museum(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: With more than half of the UK's adults are currently overweight, Cambridge geneticist Dr Giles Yeo believes that for many obese people, simply eating less is a harder than some might think. He takes a road trip around the UK and America to uncover why, meets people behind some of the shocking newspaper headlines, and through their stories, reveals truths which dispel commonly held myths about obesity(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke embark on more cultural city breaks, beginning by heading to the Portuguese capital to see what it has to offer. From a spectacular monument to the maritime globetrotting of Portugal's golden age and the work of a photographer documenting the city's large African population, they discover a complex history of former glories and a darker, slave-trading past(n)

programme.title: Our Classical Century
programme.desc: A celebration of 100 years of classical music, beginning with a look at the profound influence of the First World War on a generation of musicians and composers. From the martial might of Mars in Gustav Holst's The Planets to the pastoral beauty of Vaughan Williams' much-loved The Lark Ascending, this film tells the story of the music that brought together the United Kingdom. Presented by Suzy Klein and Lenny Henry(n)

programme.title: Love and Betrayal in India: The White Mughal
programme.desc: Historian and author William Dalrymple travels to the Indian city of Hyderabad to explore the 18th-century love affair between British diplomat James Achilles Kirkpatrick and Khair un-Nissa, the young Muslim princess he married. Dalrymple tells the story of the Kirkpatricks, and their children, through the art and architecture of the time, from the classic Georgian portraiture of George Chinnery and Thomas Hickey to the Deccani miniatures of Venkatchellam and Tajully Ali Shah(n)

programme.title: Shipwrecks: Britain's Sunken History
programme.desc: Maritime historian Dr Sam Willis reveals the stories behind some of the shipwrecks that surround Britain's coastline. He begins by examining how the top-heavy Mary Rose became a source of national embarrassment and discovers why the wrecking of the Spanish Armada was so unexpected. He also considers the terrifying real-life disasters that inspired two great castaway tales - The Tempest by William Shakespeare and Daniel Defoe's Robinson Crusoe(n)

programme.title: Queens of Soul
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage to celebrate the critically-acclaimed female singers whose voices have come to define the soul genre, including Aretha Franklin, Roberta Flack, Gladys Knight, Randy Crawford, Angie Stone, Mary J Blige and Beyonce(n)

programme.title: Metal Britannia
programme.desc: Documentary tracing the genre's emergence in the late 1960s, pioneered by bands including Black Sabbath and Judas Priest, who were based in the industrialised Midlands. This classic form of metal was challenged by punk in the late 1970s, before a new wave of groups such as Iron Maiden, Def Leppard and Saxon reinvigorated the style. Featuring contributions by singers Ian Gillan, Glenn Tipton, Lemmy, Bruce Dickinson and Rob Halford, guitarist Tony Iommi and keyboard player Jon Lord. Narrated by Nigel Planer(n)

programme.title: Slipknot Unmasked: All Out Life
programme.desc: A unique insight into the career and controversies of one of the most successful and contentious heavy metal bands of all time. The film combines new interviews, backstage access and an exclusive live session from the nine-piece group, performing six career-defining tracks at the legendary Maida Vale Studios in front of an intimate audience(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: The Hadleys and the McGregors both attend Callum's passing-in parade to the military academy, where Meggie sees a familiar face from the past and Kamal makes a controversial speech to the assembled crowd. Callum and Sephy snatch a moment together and realise they must find a place that they can be alone together - but Lekan suspects Sephy is hiding something from him and is on the rampage. programme.title: Question Time

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: In Lancashire, Michelle Ackerley learns how drone technology is helping police crack down on wildlife criminals and trace missing people. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers want to improve the planning permission in place on a terrace in Borough Green in Kent, while a mother and daughter hope they don't have too much to do on their first investment in Liverpool. Plus, a brother and sister who are seasoned property developers have ambitious plans for a generous end-of-terrace in south London's Streatham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood lines up with some serious firepower, working as a US marshal in San Antonio, Texas. His mentor is Deputy Chris Bozeman, who's spent the last 15 years arresting dangerous and wanted men. After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A plane crash in Quebec leaves a pilot stranded, an injured hiker drags himself three kilometres to safety in the Australian Outback and a brother saves his sibling who has lost consciousness on a free dive in the Bahamas. Narrated by Kevin Duala(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special edition in aid of Sport Relief in which the reds and blues are made up of Olympic legends Iwan Thomas and Tessa Sanderson, who face BBC sport presenters Manish Bhasin and John Watson. Eric Knowles hosts, while experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian offer their advice. This show features the debut of the very first Bargain Hunt games, where antique experts go head to compete for BH Sport Relief Cup. Iwan also finds time to also pay a visit to the Change Foundation, a charity that runs the London Futures project which is supported by Sport Relief.(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Five Bedrooms
programme.desc: Money worries overwhelm Liz, posing a threat to the potentially loved-up household. Drama, Starring Kat Stewart, Stephen Peacocke and Doris Younane(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in the Shropshire countryside to help a couple who have £425,000 to invest in a character home that gives them space for entertaining and their beloved jukebox. Away from the house search, Ginny visits the Royal Air Force Museum Cosford and meets the conservation team reconstructing a rare Hampden bomber which played an important role in the skies during the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades searches for three items he can rework to reveal their true potential. To help him on his quest, he enlists woodworker Ollie Allen and upholsterer Sharon O'Connor. Ollie has his hands full with a rickety old shelving unit, while Sharon needs to come up with a bright idea for a pair of garden chairs. Jay himself attempts to transform an unwanted side table(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Matched up with chef Ellis Barrie, loved-up bride-to-be Daniella is keen to rustle up some mouth-watering pasta dishes inspired by her Italian heritage. In the green pepper kitchen, budding groom Ross is paired with chef Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Paddy McGuinness, Alex Scott, Emma Willis, Rylan Clark-Neal, Oti Mabuse and Tom Allen host the extravaganza, raising funds for good causes both in the UK and overseas. Highlights include two teams of celebrities leaving it all on the dance floor in a bid to be crowned The Greatest Dancer for Sport Relief and the rebirth of Killing Eve as Killing Steves, for a one-off Sport Relief spoof. The Battle of the Channels Celebrity Boat Race returns, and this year it's bigger than ever as BBC, ITV, Channel 4 and Sky compete for victory. In another sporting feat, Romesh Ranganathan takes on tennis legend Andy Murray in a crazy game of crazy golf. Plus, live music from artists including Rita Ora, John Newman and Celeste(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Fundraising fun, live from Media City in Salford. Tom Allen and Maya Jama see viewers through to the early hours, joined by dance sensations AJ and Curtis Pritchard to get the nation up on their feet. YouTuber KSI performs a world exclusive of his single Wake Up Call, while rising star Celeste performs her new single Strange. Plus, a look back at last summer's All-Star Sport Relief Netball match with sports captains Oti Mabuse and Jennifer Saunders, a sketch with the cast of the Netflix smash hit Stranger Things and the final round of A Question of Sport Relief(n)

programme.title: The Heat Is On: Sport Relief
programme.desc: Nick Grimshaw, Frankie Bridge, Rob Rinder, Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Karim Zeroual, Samantha Womack and Louise Minchin attempt to cross the world's oldest desert in Namibia. In four days, the group must travel over 100 miles, scale the highest sand dunes on Earth and avoid deadly wildlife, all under the glare of the blistering desert sun, to finally reach the world-famous Eduard Bohlen shipwreck on Africa's skeleton coast(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Another group of six amateur cooks prepare a Signature Dish for judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace, hoping to secure their place in the next stage of the competition. programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Ryan will stop at nothing to win Faye from York back. Nick wants answers from his ex, Tiff. Tion tries to convince Aaliyah that they still have a future together. Narrated by Alice Levine(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Shell tries to prove to Laura that she is sorry for cheating on her, and Winston wants to know why Shannon ended their two-year relationship and if they warrant a second chance. Narrated by Alice Levine(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Having survived a terrifying close call with Villanelle, Eve and the team now have the mole retrieved. In a safe house, they question their prisoner, hoping to uncover information that could lead them not only to Villanelle, but also to whoever is calling the shots behind her. However, the assassin is still in the country, and none of the team are safe while she is still at large. Spy thriller, starring Jodie Comer, Sandra Oh, Fiona Shaw, Kim Bodnia and Darren Boyd(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: A female assassin is taken into custody in Russia, and Eve and Carolyn head out to Moscow to negotiate access, believing she could hold the key to the organisation behind Villanelle. Meanwhile, Villanelle is smuggled into the same Russian jail to tie up loose ends - but finds herself in a grim and brutal environment where not even she is safe. Eve fears something terrible is about to happen, and asks Kenny to spy on all communications between his mother and the Russians(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood joins the coastguard of Corpus Christi, Texas, taking to the skies with a helicopter patrol and joining a boat crew looking for illegal fishing and smuggling(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Rob go looking for evidence, while Daniel and Zara receive a rude awakening. Bear helps a patient who claims she is being stalked by a certain squeaky-voiced comedian(n)

programme.title: Five Bedrooms
programme.desc: There is a great deal of bad feeling towards Ben, who has kept some key information from his housemates. Australian drama, starring Stephen Peacocke, Kat Stewart and Doris Younane(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A fun game in the pub takes an unexpected turn when Isaac shows his true colours to Patrick. Chantelle feels the pressure to find work and Mitch's suspicions about Gray reach fever pitch. Mick is proud of Linda for going to another meeting but when Stuart spots her he realises how much she is struggling(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Restaurant critic William Sitwell sets the brief for the third quarter-final, challenging the heat-winners to serve up a plant-based dish. John Torode and Gregg Wallace then decide who will be progressing further in the competition and who will be hanging up their aprons(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mitch follows Gray to find out what he is up to - only to be stopped in his tracks by Chantelle, who warns him to back off. Stuart comes up with an extreme to cheer up Rainie - all he needs now is a ring - and Sheree berates Isaac for his attitude towards Patrick(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: The Hadleys and the McGregors both attend Callum's passing-in parade to the military academy, where Meggie sees a familiar face from the past and Kamal makes a controversial speech to the assembled crowd. Callum and Sephy snatch a moment together and realise they must find a place that they can be alone together - but Lekan suspects Sephy is hiding something from him and is on the rampage. Meanwhile, Jude is getting involved deeper with Dorn and his ambitions to make himself the new leader of terrorist group the Liberation Militia(n)

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from West Bromwich, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Newscast

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: In Lancashire, Michelle Ackerley learns how drone technology is helping police crack down on wildlife criminals and trace missing people. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers want to improve the planning permission in place on a terrace in Borough Green in Kent, while a mother and daughter hope they don't have too much to do on their first investment in Liverpool. Plus, a brother and sister who are seasoned property developers have ambitious plans for a generous end-of-terrace in south London's Streatham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood lines up with some serious firepower, working as a US marshal in San Antonio, Texas. His mentor is Deputy Chris Bozeman, who's spent the last 15 years arresting dangerous and wanted men. After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A plane crash in Quebec leaves a pilot stranded, an injured hiker drags himself three kilometres to safety in the Australian Outback and a brother saves his sibling who has lost consciousness on a free dive in the Bahamas. Narrated by Kevin Duala(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special edition in aid of Sport Relief in which the reds and blues are made up of Olympic legends Iwan Thomas and Tessa Sanderson, who face BBC sport presenters Manish Bhasin and John Watson. Eric Knowles hosts, while experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian offer their advice. This show features the debut of the very first Bargain Hunt games, where antique experts go head to compete for BH Sport Relief Cup. Iwan also finds time to also pay a visit to the Change Foundation, a charity that runs the London Futures project which is supported by Sport Relief.(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma comes under fire from the police in charge of Jimmi's case, while Al plays bad cop. Ayesha and Sid help when a patient's vasectomy leads to some shocking revelations(n)

programme.title: Five Bedrooms
programme.desc: Money worries overwhelm Liz, posing a threat to the potentially loved-up household. Drama, Starring Kat Stewart, Stephen Peacocke and Doris Younane(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in the Shropshire countryside to help a couple who have £425,000 to invest in a character home that gives them space for entertaining and their beloved jukebox. Away from the house search, Ginny visits the Royal Air Force Museum Cosford and meets the conservation team reconstructing a rare Hampden bomber which played an important role in the skies during the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades searches for three items he can rework to reveal their true potential. To help him on his quest, he enlists woodworker Ollie Allen and upholsterer Sharon O'Connor. Ollie has his hands full with a rickety old shelving unit, while Sharon needs to come up with a bright idea for a pair of garden chairs. Jay himself attempts to transform an unwanted side table(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Matched up with chef Ellis Barrie, loved-up bride-to-be Daniella is keen to rustle up some mouth-watering pasta dishes inspired by her Italian heritage. In the green pepper kitchen, budding groom Ross is paired with chef Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Paddy McGuinness, Alex Scott, Emma Willis, Rylan Clark-Neal, Oti Mabuse and Tom Allen host the extravaganza, raising funds for good causes both in the UK and overseas. Highlights include two teams of celebrities leaving it all on the dance floor in a bid to be crowned The Greatest Dancer for Sport Relief and the rebirth of Killing Eve as Killing Steves, for a one-off Sport Relief spoof. The Battle of the Channels Celebrity Boat Race returns, and this year it's bigger than ever as BBC, ITV, Channel 4 and Sky compete for victory. In another sporting feat, Romesh Ranganathan takes on tennis legend Andy Murray in a crazy game of crazy golf. Plus, live music from artists including Rita Ora, John Newman and Celeste(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Fundraising fun, live from Media City in Salford. Tom Allen and Maya Jama see viewers through to the early hours, joined by dance sensations AJ and Curtis Pritchard to get the nation up on their feet. YouTuber KSI performs a world exclusive of his single Wake Up Call, while rising star Celeste performs her new single Strange. Plus, a look back at last summer's All-Star Sport Relief Netball match with sports captains Oti Mabuse and Jennifer Saunders, a sketch with the cast of the Netflix smash hit Stranger Things and the final round of A Question of Sport Relief(n)

programme.desc: Heather Dewar travels through the parish of Gairloch in Wester Ross. programme.desc: Weekly insight into the major political, social and cultural issues hitting the headlines across Europe. In Gaelic(n)

programme.title: Robert Mone - Saorsa Gu Siorruidh?
programme.desc: The life and crimes of Dundee murderer Robert Mone, who remains active in his bid for parole(n)

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads talk to their local ladies' rugby team and prepare some after-match food on the touchline at one of their games(n)

programme.title: Fo Ghlas (World's Toughest Prisons)
programme.desc: Miami Dade County Jail, which has a protocol to break its inmates by boot camp drill(n)

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean hopes to swim through punishing currents to explore Gaylet Pot, an underground cavern near Arbroath(n)

programme.title: Fonn Fonn Fonn
programme.desc: Offbeat music trivia quiz show, hosted by Gilleasbuig Ferguson, in which guests Kimberly Roxburgh, Jamie MacDonald, Ruairidh Cormack and Anna Rachel MacDonald join team captains Carina MacLeod and Roddy Munro to test their musical knowledge, hone their listening skills and demonstrate their prowess on the kazoo. Featuring a mix of information and entertainment, bound together by the talents of in-house musician Findlay Napier(n)

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service(n)

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service(n)

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: The duo wash their car using sponges and buckets of water(n)

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: Kit & Pup investigate the world of plastic(n)

programme.title: Gudrun - A' Bhana-phrionnsa Lochlannach
programme.desc: The Viking Princess is upset when her father and Olaf the Boat Boy scare away the seals. She is baffled when, to make it up to her, King Sigurd breaks into song(n)

programme.title: Na Bleigeardan (Little Monsters)
programme.desc: Prudence discovers being messy can be fun after turning her room upside down to find her missing hairslide(n)

programme.title: Sgrìobag (Get Squiggling)
programme.desc: Squiglet draws a tortoise called Seonaidh who is on his way to a race with a speedy hare(n)

programme.title: Na Braithrean Cuideachail (The Koala Brothers)
programme.desc: Annag's sleepwalking causes mystery and confusion(n)

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Angus Peter Campbell reads Cò Mì, written by Gervase Phinn, illustrated by Tony Ross and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart(n)

programme.title: Sràid nan Sgread (Scream Street)
programme.desc: Resus is given an opportunity to show his grandfather what he isy made of when he attempts to save the town's blood supply(n)

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Lachlan from Ross-shire talks about his favourite hobbies, which include karate, swimming and playing the clàrsach(n)

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Eòseph has cerebral palsy, but is determined to walk to his own birthday party(n)

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A look at Germany in 1958, including the River Rhine and grape harvesting(n)

programme.title: Fianais
programme.desc: John Morrison talks to Torcuil Crichton about the devastation he witnessed while covering the Indian Ocean tsunami of 2004 for the Sunday Herald. Torcuil visited two of the worst affected countries, Sri Lanka and Indonesia(n)

programme.title: Togaidh Sinn Fonn (Join in the Music)
programme.desc: A selection of music by some of Scotland's most popular artists(n)

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Cases are heard when the Sheriff Court comes into session, revealing the crimes that have taken place(n)

programme.title: Thar na Loidhne/Crossing the Line
programme.desc: The Irish players deal with injuries and the Stornoway team play against their rivals, and there is a focus on Conor McMenamin, a boy from Donegal who is aiming high(n)

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell hosts the Irish-language country music show from the Town Hall in Newry, Co Down, featuring Johnny Brady and his special guest Pauline Scanlon taking the stage(n)

programme.title: Eadar na Dha: 1918-1935/Clash of Futures: 1918-1935
programme.desc: The Great War is finally over and a new era is about to start. Amidst the grief, disappointment and old fears, new dreams and hopes are now emerging but so too are new hatreds(n)

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean hopes to swim through punishing currents to explore Gaylet Pot, an underground cavern near Arbroath(n)

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: Mary Ann Kennedy with music from Celtic Connections, featuring acts including The Poozies, Two Octaves - Malin Makes Music, and Rura(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions(n)

programme.title: ITV Lunchtime News
programme.desc: Nina Hossain presents live coverage of Chancellor Rishi Sunak's Budget Statement, as ITV News correspondents and guests analyse what it means for individuals and for the nation's finances(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present the second day of the prestigious festival, including feature race the 3.30 Betway Queen Mother Champion Steeple Chase, plus races at 2.10, 2.50 and 4.10. With analysis from AP McCoy and Ruby Walsh, reports from Alice Plunkett, Brian Gleeson, Luke Harvey and Mick Fitzgerald, betting news from Matt Chapman, social stable thoughts from Oli Bell and Chris Hughes, fashion updates by Mark Heyes and Charlotte Hawkins and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the arcade-themed quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win the chance to scoop a £10,000 jackpot. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Andrew, Joe, Sara and Paddy pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Liberal Democrats(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain faces up to Malone and soon realises he is not to be messed with, Vanessa feels it is time to tell everyone about her diagnosis. Samson is over the moon when Sam asks him to do an important job(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ali assures Ryan that Gary, or someone hired by Windass, must have spiked his drink, and Alya confronts Geoff about her belief he is behind the nasty online reviews for Speed Daal. Aggie reveals that they've had several calls from newspapers wanting to get James's side of the story(n)

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp goes on a camping trip to Brownsea Island with his family, where he plans to catch and cook dinner for the whole family. After taking his grandson fishing, he heads for a cookery masterclass with head chef of the nearby Rick Stein restaurant. The next day, Harry and his son, Jamie Redknapp, compete in a game of crazy golf(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: James lies to his Manager and Press Officer and assures them he is not gay, and Ali takes Maria on a terrifying car ride. Meanwhile, Nina wonders if Roy's ashamed to be seen with her after claiming the meeting with the bat group has been cancelled(n)

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: Maggie wants to apologise to everyone she has upset, and decides to go and visit GP Carol Tomlin. The radio interview has gone viral and the village is left reeling from the revelations. Jill bonds with other fellow members of the 'outed six', Marcus and Roxanna. Meanwhile, Neil and Kelly embrace what was broadcast and pretend that they are lottery winners, and later, Maggie discovers the truth about Dr Carol's marriage. Starring Dawn French, Mark Heap, Chetna Pandya, Vicki Pepperdine and Emily Reid(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five contestants from Yorkshire answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.title: Broadchurch
programme.desc: Hardy and Ellie appeal to Trish to be more open with them as she may have information that - although not related to the attack - could help the investigation, but she is unwilling to co-operate. The officers of Broadchurch CID are slowly making their way through the painstaking process of interviewing witnesses and collecting statements and start to uncover inconsistencies and lies - and previously hidden connections between Trish and some suspects. Crime drama, with David Tennant, Olivia Colman and Julie Hesmondhalgh(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel is forced to reveal all to Jai, Malone exerts his authority over Cain, and Mandy and Dan decide to go on a date together(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: A healthy gut can do more than help digestion. It is being linked to everything from obesity levels to mental health. Five years ago actress Catherine Tyldesley made a film for Tonight about diets. Now she is back to find out exactly what people need to know about keeping the digestive system in good working order(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Samson finds a way to fund his trip, Laurel fights for her relationship with Jai, and Jamie is rattled by Andrea's custody intentions(n)

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure
programme.desc: Comedian, actor and travel enthusiast Griff Rhys Jones continues his journey across Australia on the Indian Pacific. He visits Adelaide, Broken Hill and finally Sydney to discover the unique and diverse definitions of what makes this great nation(n)

programme.title: The Station: Trouble on the Tracks
programme.desc: Documentary following the staff of Network Rail, which operates the country's railway tracks, focusing on Birmingham New Street station. Engineers battle the elements after extensive flooding, while beleaguered staff deal with stranded passengers demanding answers. Graffiti artist Banksy leaves Birmingham rail engineers a gift spray-painted on to one of the walls - all done under his usual veil of secrecy - and train manager Adam is faced with a passenger without a ticket(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Feeling the full force of DI Renton's investigation, Laura looks to those closest to her for support, but when suspicions fall on Ian, she is left questioning who she can trust. Seeking answers, she turns to a former ally, but the information she gleans only fuels her concerns. Meanwhile in the past, Oliver brings Andrew some devastating news, leading him to make a reckless and dangerous excursion. Jack and Harry Williams's thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Katherine Kelly and Sam Spruell(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby host the grand final of the skating challenge. Joe Swash, Libby Clegg and Perri Kiely and their professional partners remain in the contest, and each pair is challenged to perform a special showcase routine choreographed by Christopher Dean and Jayne Torvill. After that one pair will be eliminated, with the other going on to the final round - performing their own take on Torvill and Dean's signature piece, Ravel's Bolero. And as if that were not enough, the entire cast of this year's show will return to the ice for one last performance(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A woman is murdered in a hedgerow but her son survives the attack, only to fall beneath the ice of a frozen pond as he makes his escape. DCI Vera Stanhope wades in and drags him out, and against all the odds he pulls through. As the investigation into the mother's killing unfolds, Vera finds a kindred spirit in the boy and comes to realise the key to solving the case lies deep in his memory. Detective drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, with Kerry Fox and Kieran O'Brien(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The chalk-and-cheese detective duo are called to a private clinic where a wealthy patient has been killed. However, their investigation is made all the more difficult by the complex relationships of the victim's former fellow residents. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia take the lead roles in the second of two mystery dramas, with an all-star supporting cast including Caroline Catz, Hugh Dennis, Michele Dotrice, Freddie Fox, Suzanne Packer and Joanna Scanlan(n)

programme.desc: First to serve up a treat in Bolton, Greater Manchester, is British-born Ukrainian Myroslava, who is hoping her cultural heritage will impress her guests. programme.desc: Deacon snubs his buddies for a new black friend, so Doug swots up on African-American history in an effort to win him back. Meanwhile, Carrie tries to find out why her manicurist does not want her custom. Kevin James and Victor Williams star(n)

programme.desc: Revealing the hidden lives of the familiar and the more unusual animals in the United Kingdom. Cameras follow barn owls as they hunt for small mammals through the British countryside, and a heath potter wasp builds an intricate clay pot for her young, captured in its entirety for the very first time. Plus, the farmers doing what they can to help our countryside creatures(n)

programme.title: Are They a Con?
programme.desc: Paul Connolly and Jean Johansson investigate whether councils and the big-name supermarkets are as environmentally friendly as they claim. Paul meets a family of keen recyclers from Derbyshire whose efforts to be green may unwittingly mean their recycled waste is ending up on a landfill. The team places a tracking device inside some waste to follow exactly where the councils are sending it - and the results prove shocking. Meanwhile, Jean checks out some of the bugs that may be lurking inside the environmentally friendly water bottles people are being advised to use(n)

programme.title: Elizabeth I and II: Britain's Golden Queens
programme.desc: The concluding episode examines the importance of two women who enter the two Queen's lives; Mary, Queen of Scots and Diana, Princess of Wales. When these two young women join the Royal circle, they are expected to be great additions to the court, but in both cases, it starts to become clear that Mary and Diana's enormous popularity, beauty and sparkle are in sharp contrast to the duty-bound queens(n)

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: Documentary examining the most famous and feared criminal court in the land, and some of the cases that have been tried there, including Doctor Crippen and John Christie. The history of the building itself is also assessed, from the creation of the first Old Bailey in 1539 to its iconic facelift in 1907(n)

programme.title: Ian Huntley: Soham Murders Revisited
programme.desc: Documentary examining events that took place in the small Cambridgeshire town which horrified the country. In December 2003, Ian Huntley, a caretaker at Soham Village College, was convicted of two murders and jailed for life. This film looks at the search for his victims, Huntley's arrest, and his subsequent trial and conviction(n)

programme.title: The Chocolate Challenge with The Hairy Bikers

programme.desc: Single mother Kerry returns to her home town after receiving news that her estranged father has died. She and her daughter Ella intend to remain until the funeral, but after Ella falls in love with the town, the two decide to stay. Kerry hires a former classmate-turned-contractor to fix up her old house, but the more 'fixing' he does on his childhood crush's house, the more lethal mishaps occur. Thriller, starring Alyshia Ochse and Zac Titus(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe's half-brother makes a startling announcement and asks a great favour of her, while Joey gets involved in a class struggle. Guest starring Giovanni Ribisi(n)

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Neighbours

programme.title: Home and Away
